source: scripts/profile_hetcluster.sh; SLURM jobs 78668-78671 on partition bigTiger
method: fp32 8192x8192 matmul (throughput) + pinned 256MB H2D/D2H copies (bandwidth), torch 2.5.1+cu124
key_finding: fp32 compute is nearly FLAT across tiers (0.90x-1.51x); the binding heterogeneity is MEMORY (2.5x: 79.2/47.4/31.5 GB) and H2D PCIe bandwidth (2.15x: 57.5 vs ~26.6 GB/s). NB: RTX6000Ada is SLOWER than RTX5000Ada in fp32 matmul here -> 'bigger card' != faster; never assume, measure.
caveat: Plain fp32 CUDA-core matmul understates H100's tensor-core (TF32/FP16/FP8) advantage; re-profile with the actual GNN kernels/precision before final claims.
gnn_kernel_note: r_norm_gnn from full-batch GraphSAGE (train_smoke, jobs 78674-76, askubuntu feat128 L2). hetcluster() now uses r_norm_gnn. The GNN kernel REVERSES fp32-matmul: RTX6000 faster than RTX5000 on real workload.
